Meta Platforms announced it will build its first Canadian data center in Sturgeon County, Alberta, marking the company's 33rd data center globally. The 1-gigawatt AI-optimized facility represents an investment of more than C$13 billion and is expected to employ about 3,000 construction workers at peak activity before supporting more than 300 permanent operational jobs once completed.  Meta will invest roughly C$60 million in local infrastructure improvements, including roads and water systems, and launch its Annual Data Center Community Action Grants program to support local nonprofits. The company said it will fully fund the new power generation and grid infrastructure required for the project while covering all electricity costs. The facility's energy consumption will be matched with 100% clean and renewable energy.  The data center will also feature a closed-loop cooling system using liquid cooling and dry cooling technology.  $META

$META   So this is a how big money can move price actions.  Today AM as the market opened there was approx 6.8M shares sold: you can look at the candle. The spread was 614 to 600. This happens simply by creating a disproportionate volume. This is not new, as hedge funds or any big pocket have been doing this to move the price down.  Then they keep buying in smaller chunks to cover the short. But because all subsequent buy candles are smaller the bid spread is well contained by market maker so the price stays low..  This is a classic market manipulation but u cannot prove it since any institutions can say they were doing portfolio adjustments, delta hedging etc …  But this shitplay does not affect the big picture. $META will be soaring .. u can trust zuck for that .. who has always delivered

This challenges the narrative that Meta’s AI spending was simply overbuilding or that capex was being reduced. The company continues investing heavily in compute, data centers, and AI infrastructure to support future demand.    The bigger question: will these massive AI investments translate into higher revenue growth and efficiency gains over time?    AI infrastructure spending is not slowing yet. The race is shifting from building models to owning the compute behind them.

Mistral AI on Wednesday unveiled Robostral Navigate, a robotics navigation model that expands the French startup into physical AI following industrial partnerships with Airbus and BMW. The 8B model enables robots to navigate complex environments using a single RGB camera and simple language commands, eliminating the need for LiDAR or depth sensors.  The company said Robostral Navigate achieved a 76.6% success rate on previously unseen R2R-CE benchmarks, outperforming both the best single-camera and multi-sensor approaches. Trained entirely in simulation, the model is hardware-agnostic and supports wheeled, legged, and aerial robots across a range of sizes.  The launch strengthens Mistral's push into manufacturing, logistics, distribution, and hospitality automation as the Paris-based AI startup reportedly seeks to raise about €3 billion at a €20 billion valuation. $META  $BMWYY  $AIR
